====Pokémon====
{{MissingInfo|2|enva|java}}
This listing is of Aaron's known {{OBP|Pokémon|species}} in {{aniseries|PTS}}:
{{trainerPoké
|main=yes
|trainer=Aaron
|pkmn=Beautifly
|stage1=Wurmple
|stage2=Silcoon
|type1=Bug
|type2=Flying
|img=Aaron Beautifly.png
|epnum=DP099
|epname=A Trainer and Child Reunion!
|vajp=Kiyotaka Furushima
|vaen=Michele Knotz
|desc=Aaron first encountered his {{p|Beautifly}} as a {{p|Wurmple}} when it helped him down from a high tree. They became very close friends, but finally Aaron became irritated that it always lost in {{pkmn|battle}} and abandoned it in a park. When he later returned to find Wurmple, it was gone.

The same Wurmple returned in ''[[DP099|A Trainer and Child Reunion!]]'', where it was revealed to have since [[Evolution|evolved]] into a Beautifly. They reunited beside the very same tree where they had first met. It informed Aaron that it decided to start training by itself to become stronger. Beautifly then helped Aaron, {{Ash}}, and {{ashfr}} to battle {{TRT}} after they tried to steal Aaron's Pokémon. After this, Aaron took Beautifly back into his [[party|team]].

Beautifly reappeared in [[DP100|the next episode]], where Aaron used it as his last Pokémon in his battle against Cynthia. It went up against Cynthia's {{p|Gastrodon}}, but it was [[Fainting|knocked out]] by Gastrodon's powerful {{m|Stone Edge}}.}}

{{trainerPoké
|trainer=Aaron
|pkmn=Skorupi
|type1=Poison
|type2=Bug
|img=Aaron Skorupi.png
|epnum=DP099
|epname=A Trainer and Child Reunion!
|vajp=Katsuyuki Konishi
|desc=Aaron was seen training with a {{p|Skorupi}} in the lead-up to his match against {{an|Cynthia}}. It usually remains [[Walking Pokémon|outside of its Poké Ball]], and was seen on Aaron's shoulder.

None of Skorupi's moves are known.}}

{{trainerPoké
|trainer=Aaron
|pkmn=Drapion
|type1=Poison
|type2=Dark
|img=Aaron Drapion.png
|epnum=DP099
|epname=A Trainer and Child Reunion!
|desc={{p|Drapion}} was first seen during Aaron's training exhibition in the lead-up to his {{pkmn|battle}} with {{an|Cynthia}}. When Drapion was stolen by {{TRT}} along with Aaron's other Pokémon, it attempted to attack [[Jessie's Seviper]], but it got badly hurt by Seviper's {{m|Poison Tail}}. Later, it was able to defeat Seviper with a Hyper Beam.

During Aaron's battle against Cynthia in [[DP100|the following episode]], Drapion was sent out to face [[Cynthia's Garchomp]], but was defeated off-screen.

Drapion's known moves are {{m|Pin Missile}} and {{m|Hyper Beam}}.}}
